creator: iyersk@worldnet.att.net
text:
I installed FreeBSD 2.2.1.  The X-Server is working fine.  If I excute 
pkg_add, the system hangs.  So, I tried to go /stand/sysinstall, that time also
the system hangs.  This is happening both from prompt and from X-Windows.
If i change the dir to /cdrom, then also it hangs.  I thought  it is 
something to do with CDROM mounting. So I tried "mount" command as given
in the FReeBSD handbook, again the system hung.   I don't know the problem.  Other than
this it is working fine.  I would appreciate If anybody can help me out over this
problem.   Thanks a lot.

Iyer.

Fix: 

I don't know the fix.  But it might be with respect to mounting 
the CDROM properly.  After I installed BSD with CDROm, I took out the 
CDROM.  Is there any procedure to mount it properly.
How-To-Repeat: If /stand/sysinstall is tried from command prompt or from xwindows.
this happens.  when i try mount without any parameters, the CDROM is 
not mounted.  But the sysinstall starts first then it hangs.  This happens 
if I boot the system with the freeBSD CDROM.
